name: OpenNEM API
what_it_does: OpenNEM API provides real-time and historical electricity generation, demand, and pricing data for the Australian National Electricity Market (NEM), including renewable energy breakdown by source.
use_cases: Monitoring Australian electricity grid renewable energy percentage
Real-time electricity price tracking for demand-response applications
Historical NEM generation mix analysis for sustainability reporting
Research on Australian energy transition and coal phase-out
Carbon intensity estimation for Australian cloud/compute workloads
not_for: Individual meter or smart grid data
best_when: An agent needs free, no-auth Australian electricity data — generation mix, pricing, or renewable percentage — for research, sustainability, or monitoring applications.
avoid_when: You need a commercial SLA, non-Australian markets, or real-time data with sub-minute granularity.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Fully open API. No rate limits published; community-maintained project.

pricing.notes: Open source, community-funded project. No commercial model. Data sourced from AEMO (Australian Energy Market Operator) public feeds.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec available at /docs which helps with discovery. Error responses are basic JSON with limited context. Community-maintained documentation has gaps and may be out of date with latest API version.
known_agent_gotchas: API versioning is informal — endpoints change without formal deprecation notices
Data is in Australian Eastern time (AEST/AEDT) — agents must handle DST transitions carefully
5-minute interval data for NEM is the finest granularity; some regions only report 30-minute
Network regions use AEMO codes (NSW1, VIC1, QLD1, SA1, TAS1) — not state names
Spot prices can be highly volatile (negative to $15,000/MWh) — validate before using in calculations
Community-maintained service has had multi-day outages; no SLA exists

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No auth means no secret management burden. Open source codebase on GitHub allows dependency inspection. No sensitive data served. Community project without formal security review process.
